notes: The first-post-step 5% rule is an explicitly adaptive protocol revision made after Attempt 5 failed; no Attempt 5 arm was reused and no further relaxation is allowed.
Passing supports a bounded <=5% single-step transient and <=5% sustained recovery conclusion under this stack, not zero restart overhead.
Container data are coarse nominal 0.5 Hz trends; per-step allocator, 5 Hz GPU, and 1 Hz host evidence remain authoritative for short-lived behavior.
passed_controls: All three 60-step trajectories and canonical final model digests matched exactly within pairs.
Median paired validation-on throughput delta stayed below 5%, every data-wait fraction stayed below 1%, and all validation/scoring pause budgets passed.
Training-time and standalone scores, denominators, manifests, logical identity, window identity, and token identity matched exactly.
Cache inventories and shard hashes were unchanged, all leases released, memory recovered, following-five recovery means passed, and no swap occurred.
Every adaptive first-post-step gate passed against both pre-event and paired-off controls; all host/CUDA phase deltas are retained.
Every container adjacent-interval and per-validation-event sample gate passed alongside at least 99.5% nominal GPU and 97.9% nominal host coverage.

strict_determinism_cost_context.note: Descriptive same-stack context only; Attempt 4 failed exact trajectory and was not acceptance replication.

protocol.container_sampling_gate: at least 90% adjacent intervals <=2.5s, max <=3.0s, at least three samples inside each validation event
protocol.first_post_validation_gate: within 5% of both the preceding five-step nearest-rank p95 and the paired off-arm same step

recomputation.scope: training trajectory, final-model tensor equality, replicated validation score/identity, and training-time/standalone score/identity parity
